idea.hook: The OLDEST light in the universe is around you RIGHT NOW.
facts: This ancient light is called the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB).
It originated ~380,000 years *after* the Big Bang, 13.8 billion years ago, when the universe cooled enough for light to travel freely.
Every cubic centimeter of space, even the one between your fingertips, contains an average of ~411 CMB photons.
That means hundreds of millions of these ancient photons are passing through your body every second!
idea.payoff: You are constantly swimming in the afterglow of the universe's birth itself.
idea.title: You're Bathing in the Big Bang! 🤯

scenes.text: The OLDEST light in the universe is around you RIGHT NOW.
scenes.visual: Starts with a dark, vast space background with subtle distant stars. The view slowly zooms in slightly, creating a sense of increasing focus on the viewer's immediate surroundings.

scenes.text: This ancient light is the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB).
scenes.visual: Transition to a swirling, heat-map like visualization of the CMB, with subtle temperature variations represented by color shifts (e.g., red to blue). The visual slowly pulsates.

scenes.text: Originated ~380k years after Big Bang, when universe cooled.
scenes.visual: A simplified timeline appears, starting with a bright flash labeled 'Big Bang'. A marker quickly moves along the timeline to '~380k years', at which point the background shifts from a dense, hot plasma visual to a transparent, expanding space with light waves emanating.

scenes.text: ~411 CMB photons per cm³ pass through you, billions every second!
scenes.visual: An abstract, glowing human silhouette appears against the dark space background. Countless small, faint glowing particles (photons) are shown continuously streaming *through* the silhouette from all directions.

scenes.text: You are constantly swimming in the afterglow of the universe's birth itself.
scenes.visual: The human silhouette dissolves, replaced by a grand, awe-inspiring view of a nebula or a galaxy, subtly overlaid with the CMB heat-map pattern. The entire scene glows gently, emphasizing the pervasive nature of the CMB.

youtube.title: You're Bathing in the Big Bang! 🤯
